perturbation.original_text: Section 1.01 Appointment. Lessor hereby appoints the Company, and the Company hereby agrees to accept such appointment, as the agent of Lessor, without any fee for acting as such agent, pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, for the purpose of ordering and, subject to the conditions set forth in Section's 2.01 and 2.05 hereof, accepting Equipment on Lessor's behalf for leasing to the Company under the Lease from the respective supplier thereof (each a "Supplier" and collectively, the "Suppliers"). It is specifically agreed that all of the power and authority vested to the Company herein shall be subject to any modifications as may from time to time be made by Lessor.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 1.01 Appointment. Lessor hereby appoints the Company, and the Company hereby agrees to accept such appointment, as the exclusive agent of Lessor, with a fee of 1% of the total equipment cost for acting as such agent, pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, for the purpose of ordering and, subject to the conditions set forth in Section's 2.01 and 2.05 hereof, accepting Equipment on Lessor's behalf for leasing to the Company under the Lease from the respective supplier thereof (each a "Supplier" and collectively, the "Suppliers"). It is specifically agreed that all of the power and authority vested to the Company herein shall be subject to any modifications as may from time to time be made by Lessor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces two contradictions. First, the Company is now the 'exclusive' agent, whereas elsewhere in the agreement, the possibility of other agents isn't explicitly excluded, implying a non-exclusive relationship. Second, a fee of 1% is introduced, conflicting with the original statement of 'without any fee'. This creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement elsewhere states the Company acts as an agent 'without any fee'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 1.01 Appointment. Lessor hereby appoints the Company, and the Company hereby agrees to accept such appointment, as the exclusive agent of Lessor, with a fee of 1% of the total equipment cost for acting as such agent, pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, for the purpose of ordering and, subject to the conditions set forth in Section's 2.01 and 2.05 hereof, accepting Equipment on Lessor's behalf for leasing to the Company under the Lease from the respective supplier thereof (each a "Supplier" and collectively, the "Suppliers"). It is specifically agreed that all of the power and authority vested to the Company herein shall be subject to any modifications as may from time to time be made by Lessor.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces two direct in-text contradictions. The original text states 'without any fee,' while the changed text introduces 'a fee of 1%'. This creates a financial contradiction. Furthermore, it changes the agent from potentially non-exclusive to 'exclusive,' which implies that there can be no other agents that Lessor hires, triggering an in-text contradiction, as the agreement does not explicitly exclude other agents from being hired previously.

perturbation.original_text: Section 2.01 Equipment Orders. Upon the written acknowledgment by the Company and Lessor of each jointly approved purchase agreement, purchase order or invoice ("Purchase Order"), the Company, pursuant to the agency granted to it by Lessor in Article I hereof, may order, receive, accept the Equipment to be leased in accordance with the Economic Terms (as hereafter defined and described). Upon and as of the date of acceptance of the Equipment by the Company and satisfaction of the conditions precedent provided for in the Lease: (a) Lessor shall be unconditionally obligated to purchase such Equipment pursuant to the terms of the applicable Purchase Order and to lease such Equipment to the Company pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule; and (b) the Company shall be unconditionally obligated to lease such Equipment from Lessor pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 2.01 Equipment Orders. Upon the verbal acknowledgment by either the Company or Lessor of each purchase agreement, purchase order or invoice ("Purchase Order"), the Company, pursuant to the agency granted to it by Lessor in Article I hereof, may order, receive, accept the Equipment to be leased at its sole discretion. Upon and as of the date of acceptance of the Equipment by the Company and satisfaction of the conditions precedent provided for in the Lease: (a) Lessor shall be obligated to purchase such Equipment pursuant to the terms of the applicable Purchase Order and to lease such Equipment to the Company pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule; and (b) the Company shall be obligated to lease such Equipment from Lessor pursuant to the terms and conditions of the Lease and the applicable completed Schedule.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces contradictions. Firstly, the approval process is changed from 'written acknowledgement by the Company and Lessor of each jointly approved purchase agreement' to 'verbal acknowledgment by either the Company or Lessor'. This contradicts the implicit requirement for joint decision-making elsewhere in the agreement by giving power to either party individually. Additionally, the 'Economic Terms' clause is not being considered, as the Company can order, receive, and accept equipment at its sole discretion. This introduces ambiguity about whether adherence to jointly approved purchase agreements or Economic Terms is required, affecting the contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 2.05 Economic Terms. The Company and the Lessor hereby agree that Schedules entered into pursuant to this Agency Agreement shall conform with the following "Economic Terms": 1. Maximum Aggregate Capitalized Lessor's Cost: $14,500,000.00 2. Basic Term Lease Rate Factor: To be mutually agreed upon by Company and Lessor 3. Basic Term (No. of Months): To be mutually agreed upon by Company and Lessor 4. Equipment Type: To be mutually agreed upon by Company and Lessor 5. Agency Agreement Expiration Date and Last Delivery Date: October 31, 2006
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates 'written acknowledgment by the Company and Lessor of each jointly approved purchase agreement'. The changed text replaces this with 'verbal acknowledgment by either the Company or Lessor,' allowing a single party to approve purchase agreements, and removes the need to conform to Economic Terms. This directly contradicts Section 2.05, which requires 'Economic Terms' such as 'Basic Term Lease Rate Factor' and 'Equipment Type' to be 'mutually agreed upon by Company and Lessor.' The change undermines the mutual agreement requirement, creating a strong in-text contradiction as the company can now act at its sole discretion.

perturbation.original_text: Section 3.01 Termination. (a) So long as no default exists and is continuing hereunder or under the Lease, either party may terminate this Agreement at any time upon ____________ (______30________) days written notice to the other party; provided however that such termination shall not act as a termination of any Equipment leased hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 3.01 Termination. (a) Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without any prior notice, for any reason whatsoever; provided however that such termination shall act as a termination of any Equipment leased hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original termination clause. Previously, termination required 30 days' written notice and did not affect existing equipment leases. Now, termination can occur immediately without notice but terminates all existing Equipment leases. This creates uncertainty and an in-text contradiction because the original clause protected existing leases, while the modified clause voids them upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 3.01 Termination. (a) Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without any prior notice, for any reason whatsoever; provided however that such termination shall act as a termination of any Equipment leased hereunder.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a direct contradiction. The original text stated that termination required 30 days written notice and did not terminate existing equipment leases. The changed text removes the notice period, allows immediate termination, and terminates the equipment leases, which directly contradicts the obligations and protections stated in the original clause. The changed text creates ambiguities and inconsistencies in how termination affects the existing equipment leases, which is defined in the original agreement.
